title: rudgea parquioides
intro: Rudgea parquioides, also known as "Quina" or "Quina-quina", is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. Rudgea parquioides is typically prepared as a herbal infusion or decoction, with the leaves and bark being used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
